question.task_question:
You are an agent that can predict future numeric market outcomes. The event to be predicted:
"""
During the trading week containing 2026-05-13 (UTC+8), what will be the highest daily Brent crude oil spot price, in USD per barrel, reported by FRED series DCOILBRENTEU?
"""

Resolve this by taking the maximum released FRED DCOILBRENTEU daily observation whose observation date falls inside the week. Ignore weekends or holidays with no released observation.

Your goal is to make a numeric prediction.

IMPORTANT: Your final answer MUST end with this exact format:
\boxed{number}

The number must be USD/bbl rounded to two decimals. Do not use any other final format.
question.task_description: Demo fixture for the Forecast page numeric surface. Resolution source is FRED series DCOILBRENTEU; Hormuz evidence may inform risk premium, but Market data is evidence input only and must be separated from unresolved maritime-flow claims.

actions.summary: Brent weekly high numeric question loaded for the Hormuz case.

rawPreview.title: Forecast question
rawPreview.text:
You are an agent that can predict future numeric market outcomes. The event to be predicted:
"""
During the trading week containing 2026-05-13 (UTC+8), what will be the highest daily Brent crude oil spot price, in USD per barrel, reported by FRED series DCOILBRENTEU?
"""

Resolve this by taking the maximum released FRED DCOILBRENTEU daily observation whose observation date falls inside the week. Ignore weekends or holidays with no released observation.

Your goal is to make a numeric prediction.

IMPORTANT: Your final answer MUST end with this exact format:
\boxed{number}

The number must be USD/bbl rounded to two decimals. Do not use any other final format.

actions.title: Question audit and search plan
actions.summary: Identify the FRED target series, recent Brent band, and Hormuz risk-premium context.

rawPreview.title: Assistant note
rawPreview.text: [demo] Plan: verify FRED DCOILBRENTEU target, inspect recent Brent levels, then add limited Hormuz risk context without treating it as resolution data.

toolCalls.name: search_web
toolCalls.arguments: {"query":"FRED DCOILBRENTEU Brent crude oil spot price","domains":["fred.stlouisfed.org"]}

toolCalls.name: search_web
toolCalls.arguments: {"query":"Strait of Hormuz maritime advisory Brent risk premium","domains":["eia.gov","ukmto.org"]}

actions.title: Search FRED target series
actions.summary: FRED DCOILBRENTEU Brent crude oil spot price target lookup.

actions.title: Search Hormuz risk context

actions.title: Hormuz context result

rawPreview.title: Assistant note
rawPreview.text: [demo] Refine the query around current-week Brent highs; prediction will be grounded on the local FRED latest observation.

forecastPayload.rationale: [demo] 119.44 USD/bbl is derived from the latest FRED DCOILBRENTEU local observation (118.26 on 2026-05-01) plus a 1 % demo risk-premium; replace with a real LLM run for a substantive forecast.
keyEvidenceItems: [demo] FRED DCOILBRENTEU latest local observation is 118.26 USD/bbl on 2026-05-01.
[demo] FRED DCOILBRENTEU recent 20-day range is 98.63–138.21 USD/bbl; 119.44 USD/bbl is inside this band.
[demo] Official maritime-advisory context remains a risk-premium input, not the resolution source.
counterEvidenceItems: [demo] Without a fresh verified closure-class traffic stop, the demo risk premium is assumed to be small (~1 %).
openConcerns: [demo] Real LLM run should replace this fixture before any substantive forecast review.
temporalNotes: [demo] Weekly high resolves after all FRED DCOILBRENTEU observations in the target week are released.

evidenceClaims.claim: [demo] Local FRED DCOILBRENTEU observations ground the sparkline and delta; the demo prediction (119.44 USD/bbl) is derived from the latest FRED observation + 1% risk-premium.
[demo] Hormuz chokepoint context supports a modest risk-premium input, not an extreme spike.
[demo] Pending live-flow evidence argues against presenting this fixture as a closure-shock Brent spike.
